ctxMarket: Attention is on sharp corrections across Solana ecosystem tokens, with JTO dropping over 13% and Solana futures open interest down 30% in May. Despite price declines, Solana’s on-chain activity and application revenue remain strong.
ctxTalking:
• Jito’s significant price drop and concerns about further corrections in JTO
• A targeted TrapDoor malware campaign stealing wallet and credential data from Solana, Sui, and Aptos developers
• New sports industry integrations on Solana, including fantasy, prediction markets, and fan tokens
ctxMatters: Recent token volatility may impact confidence and liquidity for users and builders. Security threats targeting developer tools highlight ongoing risks for wallet and credential safety. Continued growth in application revenue and new industry integrations suggest persistent developer and user engagement on Solana.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Sustained high Solana activity and revenue despite market downturns; ongoing security threats to developer infrastructure.
Noise: Speculation around specific price targets for SOL without clear catalysts.
ctxGlossary:
Open Interest — The total value of outstanding futures contracts not yet settled.
TrapDoor — A malware campaign targeting developer credentials and wallets via fake software packages.

readMore.title: Jito’s crash wipes out gains – 13.27% drop raises JTO’s correction fears
Solana, Sui and Aptos wallet data targeted in TrapDoor package attack
Solana open interest drops 30% as altcoins slump
